title: Aster gymnocephalus
taxonomy: Aster gymnocephalus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a part of the genus Aster, which includes over 600 species of daisies and related plants. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central Europe, where it grows in rocky and stony areas. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Aster gymnocephalus is closely related to other plants such as sunflowers and dandelions. The classification of Aster gymnocephalus is further divided into its binomial nomenclature, with the full species name being Aster gymnocephalus.

intro: Aster gymnocephalus, also known as Naked Daisy, is a perennial plant species in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system and digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Aster gymnocephalus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aster gymnocephalus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and its dried flowers are used in herbal formulas to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
